channel.title: Night Owl
channel.description: Automatic update feed for Night Owl.

item.title: Version 1.1 (build 3)
item.description:
Night Owl 1.1 (build 3)

  • AI processing suggestions — the Process tab has a new “Suggest settings” button that asks Claude to recommend stacking, drizzle, gradient/color, stretch, and BlurX/NoiseX/StarX settings tuned to a target's integration time and scope. Add your own Anthropic API key in Settings ▸ AI (stored only in your Keychain).
  • Fixes a first-run processing failure — the integration step could fail the first time it ran because its output folder hadn't been created yet. It's now created up front, with a clear error if that ever fails.
  • Sky map search & add-from-sky — search or go to any object on the sky map, and add a target directly from where the map is centered.
  • Live sky conditions — the Conditions tab now shows live weather plus aurora/geomagnetic (Kp) activity and a dew-point margin, with an optional alert when a strong geomagnetic storm is forecast.
  • Cleaner Tracker — removed the “verified hours” line that wasn't reporting reliably; per-night integration totals are unchanged.

item.title: Version 1.1
item.description:
Night Owl 1.1

  • Reclaims disk space on the Workstation — after a night's verified keepers are archived to your external drive, the local working copies are removed automatically, so the processing folder no longer grows without bound.
  • Process from your archive — on the Workstation, the Process tab now works from archived target folders (Add Target Folder…), with integration hours read from the folder itself rather than imported sessions whose files have been reclaimed.
  • Settings tailored to each node — the Settings panel now hides options that don't apply to the selected node type.

item.title: Version 1.0
item.description:
Night Owl 1.0

First release distributed with built-in automatic updates.

  • In-app updates: Night Owl now checks for new versions and updates itself.
  • Accurate capture hours: integration time is reconciled per observation night, so a target's total reflects every night it was imaged.
  • Sky map, plate-solve pointing checks, and mosaic planning.
